title: Bookings vs. Deployments
query: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM BookDropship WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS bookings, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM DropshipDeploy WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS deployments

details.text: {bookings} dropship booking(s) made vs. {deployments} actual deployment(s) during the report period. The gap between bookings and deployments reflects cancelled operations, rethought plans, or missions that fell apart before launch.
details.help: bookings is the primary metric as it captures intent. deployments shows execution. A clean 1:1 ratio indicates disciplined mission planning.

tagLines: Dropship utilisation rate calculated. Operational planning efficiency noted by logistics command.
Plans are made in safety. Deployments happen in chaos. The ratio tells you which won.
Every cancelled booking is a battle you chose not to fight. Sometimes that's wisdom.
